name: Evergy, Inc.
business: Evergy is a public utility holding company that generates, transmits, distributes, and sells electricity to approximately 1.7 million customers in Kansas and Missouri, with a total generating capacity of approximately 15,800 megawatts.

exposures.sentence: Evergy is a public utility holding company incorporated in 2017 and headquartered in Kansas City, Missouri.

In February 2026, the Evergy Companies signed electric service agreements (ESAs) with multiple large load customers to serve data centers with a projected peak steady state load of approximately 1,900 MWs.

Evergy Transmission Company owns 13.5% of Transource Energy, LLC (Transource) with the remaining 86.5% owned by AEP Transmission Holding Company, LLC, a subsidiary of American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP). Transource is focused on the development of competitive electric transmission projects.

Evergy has an indirect 94% ownership interest in Wolf Creek Generating Station (Wolf Creek), which is subject to regulation by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) with respect to licensing, operations and safety-related requirements.
The actual 2025 fuel mix and fuel cost in cents per net kilowatt hour (kWh) delivered are outlined in the following table. Fuel Mix (a) Actual 2025: Wind, landfill gas and solar (b) 28%

targeting approximately $21.6 billion of expected capital investments through 2030 including new generation of approximately $9.3 billion which is expected to be primarily natural gas, renewable generation and battery storage capacity in support of historic economic development opportunities in Kansas and Missouri.
In October 2024, Evergy announced its plan to construct two combined-cycle natural gas plants located in Kansas.

catalysts.event: Evergy Kansas Central new retail rates effective
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, the KCC approved the unanimous settlement agreement and new rates took effect in October 2025.

catalysts.event: Evergy Missouri West new retail rates effective
catalysts.sentence: new Evergy Missouri West retail rates effective in January 2025
Wolf Creek's next refueling outage is planned to begin in the spring of 2027.

catalysts.event: Sumner County CCGT plant begins operations
catalysts.sentence: The first plant, a combined cycle gas turbine (CCGT) facility located in Sumner County, is expected to begin operations by spring of 2029

catalysts.event: Reno County CCGT plant begins operations
catalysts.sentence: the second plant, a CCGT facility located in Reno County, is expected to begin operations by spring of 2030

catalysts.event: Missouri simple-cycle natural gas plant begins operations
catalysts.sentence: Additionally, Evergy Missouri West plans to construct a 440 MW simple-cycle natural gas plant located in Missouri. The plant is expected to begin operations in 2030.

catalysts.event: Sunflower Sky and Foxtrot solar facilities begin operations
catalysts.sentence: The solar generation facilities are expected to begin operations by summer of 2027.

catalysts.event: Large load customer ESAs service start
catalysts.sentence: The ESAs' terms reflect the applicable provisions of the Evergy Companies LLPS rate plans and the service of these large load customers, inclusive of a 5-year transitional load period, is expected to begin at dates ranging from 2026 to 2028.
